key_insight: The Large Cancer Assistant (LCA) is a model-agnostic orchestration layer that separates oncology data routing from AI inference to make multimodal clinical decision support more flexible and scalable.
clinical_relevance: If validated further, this architecture could help hospitals integrate changing AI models and multimodal patient data more safely without rewriting the full decision-support pipeline.
method: Formalized LCA as a 7-tuple architecture built on “algorithmic impermeability.”
Used an Entry Theory with geometric deep learning to standardize multimodal patient data.
Routed data through a Cancer Switching Module and emitted a standardized intermediate payload (SIP) to isolate AI execution from hospital IT systems.
evidence_signal: The proof-of-concept tested four technical scenarios and reported negligible orchestration overhead, invariant routing during model swaps, and 100% recall for targeted supplementary data requests under injected anomalies.
watch_item: The framework was only demonstrated in proof-of-concept settings, so real-world interoperability, robustness, and clinical workflow impact still need validation.
paper_title: The Large Cancer Assistant (LCA): A Model-Agnostic Orchestration Framework for Scalable Clinical Decision Support in Oncology
